title: Reading Music
description: The ability to accurately read, internalize, and perform sheet music in real-time.

levels.title: Basic Clefs & Rhythms
levels.masteryCriteria: The student can confidently identify notes residing strictly within the five lines of either the Treble or Bass clef. They can maintain a steady pulse while performing whole, half, and quarter note rhythms.

levels.title: The Grand Staff & Simple Meter
levels.masteryCriteria: The student can transition fluidly between the treble and bass clefs across the grand staff. They understand fundamental dynamics (forte, piano) and perform eighth-note subdivisions cleanly within 4/4 and 3/4 time.

levels.title: Expanded Range & Syncopation
levels.masteryCriteria: The student effortlessly reads outside the standard staves up to two ledger lines. They handle accidental alterations (sharps and flats) on the fly, and can perform syncopated figures involving dotted notes and precise 16th note groupings.

levels.title: Advanced Meter
levels.masteryCriteria: The student naturally feels the macro-beat in compound meters like 6/8 and 9/8, seamlessly differentiating between triplet stress patterns and straight eighth groupings.

title: Applied Music Theory
description: The structural understanding of harmony, scales, intervals, and tonality.

levels.title: The Foundation of Tonality
levels.masteryCriteria: The student can quickly determine the numeric distance between any two notes on a staff and understands the specific whole/half step formula required to construct a standard Major scale.

levels.title: Keys & Diatonic Harmony
levels.masteryCriteria: The student can instantly identify a major key from any sharp or flat key signature. Given a root note, they can rapidly stack thirds to construct flawless Major and minor triads.

levels.title: Advanced Tonality & Voicing
levels.masteryCriteria: The student understands the concept of relative minor keys and can deduce them from a Major key signature. They can rapidly identify basic triads regardless of whether the root, third, or fifth is in the bass voice.
